title: eucalyptus pauciflora subsp. hedraia
intro: Eucalyptus pauciflora subsp. hedraia, commonly known as Alpine Cider Gum or Miena Gum, is a subspecies of eucalyptus native to the mountainous regions of Tasmania, Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, making it a popular natural remedy for colds, coughs, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eucalyptus pauciflora subsp. hedraia include relief from congestion, sinusitis, and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as essential oils, teas, and salves, which can be used topically or inhaled to harness its medicinal properties.
